hook.text: API keys, database passwords, tokens — your application is full of secrets. Where do you store them safely?

buildup.text: Never hardcode secrets in source code. A single leaked repo exposes every credential your app uses.

discovery.text: Secret managers like AWS Secrets Manager, Azure Key Vault, and HashiCorp Vault store and rotate secrets.

twist.text: GitHub scans billions of commits daily and finds thousands of exposed secrets. Most are still active.

climax.text: Automated secret scanning and rotation are table stakes for cloud security. Manual management doesn't scale.

punchline.text: Secrets in code are public. Use a vault. Always.

quiz.question: Why should secrets never be hardcoded in source code?
options: A leaked repository exposes all credentials the application uses
Hardcoded secrets make applications run slower
Source code can only store strings shorter than 20 characters
